refnamediv.refpurpose: Compute the Euler number of a binary image.

listitem.para: Two-dimensional Boolean or numeric binary image. Nonzero pixels represent foreground objects.
Pixel connectivity used to identify objects and holes: 4 or 8. The default is 8.
Scalar Euler number, computed as the number of connected foreground objects minus the number of enclosed background holes.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: The Euler number describes the topology of a binary image. A solid object contributes +1, while each hole contributes -1. For example, three objects containing three holes have Euler number 3 - 3 = 0.

refsection.title: Examples
Reference
refsection.para: The example illustrates the Euler-number definition documented by the MATLAB Image Processing Toolbox bweuler reference using an independently constructed IPCV image.
